---
title: "Enterprise Agents Wiki"
subtitle: "An engineering research manual for building AI-native products."
---

This wiki exists to turn agent product ideas into buildable contracts.

It is not a collection of essays. Each accepted page should define claims, sources, boundaries, records, diagrams, failure modes, and tests.

[Research operating system](research-operating-system.md){.btn .btn-primary}
[System diagram spine](system-diagram-spine.md){.btn .btn-secondary}

## Current Position

The product problem:

```text
Agents should not be bolted onto products as chat boxes.
They should become governed product actors with explicit authority, tools, workflows, memory, audit, evals, and release controls.
```

The engineering rule:

```text
The agent coordinates work.
The product owns authority and records.
The workflow owns side-effect recovery.
The source systems own truth.
The audit log owns accountability.
The eval and release process owns improvement.
```

## Canonical Architecture

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  Surface["Work surface"] --> Context["Context manifest"]
  Context --> Access["Access decision"]
  Access --> Runtime["Agent runtime"]
  Runtime --> Gateway["Capability gateway"]
  Gateway --> Policy["Policy decision"]
  Policy --> Approval["Approval record"]
  Approval --> Workflow["Durable workflow"]
  Workflow --> Source["Source response"]
  Source --> Verify["Verification result"]
  Verify --> Timeline["Product timeline"]
  Verify --> Audit["Audit event"]
  Verify --> Eval["Eval case"]
  Eval --> Release["Release bundle"]
```

Every layer must answer:

| Question | Required artifact |
|---|---|
| What boundary is this? | Architecture decision or system contract |
| What proves the claim? | Claim ID and source evidence |
| What records are written? | Schema, ERD, event, or API contract |
| What can fail? | Negative case and recovery path |
| What blocks release? | Eval fixture or acceptance test |

## Read First

| Order | Page | Why |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | [Research operating system](research-operating-system.md) | Defines the quality bar: claim ledger, evidence classes, page gates, rewrite queue. |
| 2 | [Site template decision](site-template-decision.md) | Explains why Quarto remains the base and why the old HTML lab is demoted. |
| 3 | [System diagram spine](system-diagram-spine.md) | Establishes the ERD, state machine, sequence, topology, correlation, and policy diagrams. |
| 4 | [Architecture blueprint](architecture-blueprint.md) | Gives the current layer model and minimum vertical slice. |
| 5 | [Implementation lab](implementation-lab.md) | Should become the canonical backend contract spec. |
| 6 | [References](references.md) and [Source atlas](source-atlas.md) | Current source library; needs conversion into claim-level registry. |

## Build Path

The first real implementation target is a fake but contract-complete bed-flow run:

```text
POST /agent-runs
-> bind ContextManifest
-> create AccessDecision
-> plan TaskGraph
-> read source evidence
-> preview reserve_bed
-> require exact Approval
-> start durable WorkflowRun
-> store SourceResponse
-> create VerificationResult
-> write TimelineEvent and AuditEvent
-> sample EvalCase
```

Acceptance:

- ambiguous context stops before source reads
- denied scope produces no tool call
- malformed payload is rejected by schema
- approval binds to canonical payload hash
- rejected approval never starts workflow
- workflow retry cannot duplicate a side effect
- source mismatch becomes `needs_reconciliation`
- patient-specific facts are not stored as durable memory
- release is blocked by failed approval, memory, denial, or source-truth evals

## What Changed

The old [interactive app](interactive.html) is no longer the center of the wiki. It is now a derived lab surface indexed at [Interactive labs](interactive-labs.md). Deep links are kept because some simulations are useful, but durable knowledge must move into markdown, diagrams, schemas, and tests.

## Rewrite Priorities

| Priority | Work | Definition of done |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Source registry | `references.md` becomes a claim-level source registry with citation keys, dates, evidence class, caveats, and supported claims. |
| 2 | Backend contracts | `implementation-lab.md` defines JSON Schemas, API contracts, event payloads, lifecycle enums, and error responses. |
| 3 | Identity and grants | `identity-access-lab.md` and `capability-registry.md` define a deterministic grant-resolution algorithm and denial taxonomy. |
| 4 | Approval boundary | `approval-handoff.md` defines state transitions, canonical payload hashing, expiry, modification, escalation, and resume-token rules. |
| 5 | Eval harness | `eval-release-harness.md` defines fixtures, trajectory assertions, release thresholds, reports, and CI integration. |
| 6 | Threat model | `agent-threat-model.md` replaces simulated debate with threat cases, controls, denial evals, evidence records, and incident paths. |

## Template Direction

We stay with Quarto for now because the core artifact is research-heavy markdown with diagrams, citations, sidebars, search, and static publishing. Astro/Starlight remains a candidate only if the site becomes a component-driven docs app with many focused interactive islands.
